text.title: dev-methodologies-architecture Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for dev-methodologies-architecture. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use expand-then-contract for every schema change in a CD pipeline: first add the new column/table (expand), migrate data, then remove the old column (contract) in a separate deploy.
rule.rationale: Combining add + drop in one migration causes zero-downtime deployment to fail: the app version that reads the new column deploys while old pods still query the old schema.

rule.statement: Run EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) on every suspected slow query on representative data volume; add an index only after confirming the plan switches from Seq Scan to Index Scan.
rule.rationale: Indexes on write-heavy columns raise write cost; adding indexes before profiling may not change the query plan if statistics are stale or row counts are small.

rule.statement: Prefetch related objects in ORM queries that iterate over a collection; write a query-count assertion in tests to catch N+1 regressions.
rule.rationale: An N+1 that works fine in dev with 10 rows produces thousands of queries in production. Query-count assertions catch regressions far cheaper than profiling later.

rule.statement: Centralize the API response envelope in one module (Pydantic model or TypedDict); success responses wrap data under a data key, errors under an error key with code, message, and details.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent envelope shapes force every API consumer to handle different structures; a single module makes the shape a code-reviewable contract that prevents drift.

rule.statement: Choose auth pattern based on statefulness requirement: JWT for stateless API auth, session cookies for server-side state, OAuth2 for third-party login, API keys for server-to-server.
rule.rationale: Using JWT where session revocation is needed (e.g., security incident) requires a token blacklist, which negates the stateless advantage.

rule.statement: Never let an agent rotate production signing keys, OAuth client secrets, or password-hash parameters; those changes require human approval and coordinated deployment.
rule.rationale: Auth key rotation is a coordinated security operation; autonomous rotation without a deployment window invalidates all active sessions and can lock users out.

rule.statement: For cache-aside (the most common pattern), always pair set+get with a documented TTL, jitter (TTL ± 10%), and a single-flight guard against stampede.
rule.rationale: Without jitter, a cache expiry wave causes all callers to miss simultaneously and hammer the database. Without single-flight, N concurrent misses each trigger a DB query.

rule.statement: Store idempotency keys and created_at on every background job; design job handlers to be idempotent so failed jobs can be re-driven without duplication.
rule.rationale: Networks and workers fail; a non-idempotent job re-driven after failure charges customers twice or sends duplicate emails.

rule.statement: Never let an agent promote a replica autonomously; require human approval for any failover or topology change.
rule.rationale: Automatic promotion without quorum check risks split-brain: both primary and promoted replica accept writes, leading to diverged data that is difficult to reconcile.

rule.statement: Every log entry must include: event (snake_case verb), actor (who triggered it), object (what was affected), latency_ms (for I/O operations), and correlation_id (request or trace ID).
rule.rationale: Without a standard field set, logs are unsearchable in Datadog/Grafana. The field contract must be documented and code-reviewed just like an API schema.

rule.statement: Use INFO for expected business events, WARNING for recoverable anomalies, ERROR for actionable failures, and CRITICAL for service-threatening conditions only.
rule.rationale: Over-logging at ERROR saturates alerting and desensitizes on-call responders; under-logging at INFO hides the breadcrumb trail needed for post-incident analysis.

rule.statement: Enforce no print() in production code via ruff T20 rule; agents that add print() for debugging must remove it before committing.
rule.rationale: print() bypasses the log aggregation stack, lacks structured fields, and clutters stdout in containers where stdout is parsed as structured JSON.
